reasoning: As of late March 2025, multiple defense and mainstream media outlets (including Yonhap, Overt Defense, and Army Recognition) have reported that South Korea and Vietnam have either finalized or are "set to purchase" a $276 million (400 billion won) deal for 20 K9 self-propelled howitzers. The reporting highlights that the exact price, quantity, and specific vehicle model (K9A1) are all agreed upon, with Vietnamese personnel already having undergone training in late 2024. Although an unmistakable official government press release (e.g., from DAPA) was not readily found prior to the May 1 cutoff, the continuous stream of confident reporting citing government sources and even occasionally calling it "signed" suggests the deal is a near-certainty. Delays are always possible in international arms procurement, but the highly advanced stage of these negotiations gives a high probability that an official announcement will occur before the July 1, 2025 deadline.
